articles.title: ExxonMobil legal move to Texas, new name expected July 1
articles.summary: Exxon Mobil Corporation's redomiciliation from New Jersey to Texas is set to take effect on July 1, with ExxonMobil Holdings Corporation becoming the new publicly traded parent company. Shareholders approved the move on May 27, and the company's shares will continue to trade o...
articles.content:
Exxon Mobil Corporation said its redomiciliation from New Jersey to Texas is expected to take effect July 1, when ExxonMobil Holdings Corporation will become the publicly traded parent company.

The company said in a news release that ExxonMobil Holdings Corporation will replace Exxon Mobil Corporation of New Jersey as the publicly traded parent company. Shares will continue to trade on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol XOM, and shareholders are not required to take any action.

Advertisement

Article continues below this ad

ExxonMobil shareholders approved the move May 27, with 71.2% of votes cast in favor.

The resolution was approved with 71% of votes cast, with local refineries supporting the motion.

ExxonMobil said it expects to file a Form 8-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission after the redomiciliation is completed.

UTWO is part of the first single-bond ETF suite. The targeted holding makes it very different from other ETFs holding a basket of 2-year Treasury notes. This is a tool used in portfolio management. The fund tracks an index that holds just the on-the-run 2-year US Treasury notes, which are the most recently issued and most liquid. At each monthly rebalancing, the underlying issue is sold and rolled into a newly selected issue, given that there has been a new public sale or auction by the US Government for 2-year Treasury notes. This roll transition occurs on one day, each month. The fund pays transaction costs when it buys and sells securities. These costs are not reflected in the annual fund operating expenses and should be expected. The fund will experience a very high turnover. This may result in higher taxes, as compared to other ETFs, if the shares are held in a taxable account. Prior to April 7, 2025, the fund tracked the ICE BofA Current 2-Year US Treasury Index. Effective July 1, 2025, the fund appended F/m to its fund name.

An exchange-traded fund (ETF) is a collection of assets (stocks, bonds, commodities, etc.) that track an underlying index and can be bought on an exchange like individual stocks.

In some ways, ETFs are safe investments, but in a broader sense, they're not safer than any other asset, so it's crucial to analyze a fund before investing. But if your research gives a vague answer, you can always refer to technical analysis.

See more of UTWO technicals for a more comprehensive analysis.

Yes, UTWO pays dividends to its holders with the dividend yield of 3.49%.

UTWO trades at a premium (0.00%).
Premium/discount to NAV expresses the difference between the ETF’s price and its NAV value. A positive percentage indicates a premium, meaning the ETF trades at a higher price than the calculated NAV. Conversely, a negative percentage indicates a discount, suggesting the ETF trades at a lower price than NAV.

Premium/discount to NAV expresses the difference between the ETF’s price and its NAV value. A positive percentage indicates a premium, meaning the ETF trades at a higher price than the calculated NAV. Conversely, a negative percentage indicates a discount, suggesting the ETF trades at a lower price than NAV.

UTWO shares are issued by F/m Investments LLC

UTWO follows the Bloomberg US Treasury Bellwether 2Y Index - Benchmark TR Gross. ETFs usually track some benchmark seeking to replicate its performance and guide asset selection and objectives.

The fund started trading on Aug 9, 2022.

The fund's management style is passive, meaning it's aiming to replicate the performance of the underlying index by holding assets in the same proportions as the index. The goal is to match the index's returns.

articles.content:
When Jeff Bezos said that one breakthrough technology would shape Amazon’s destiny, even Wall Street’s biggest analysts were caught off guard.

Fast forward a year and Amazon’s new CEO Andy Jassy described generative AI as a “once-in-a-lifetime” technology that is already being used across Amazon to reinvent customer experiences.

At the 8th Future Investment Initiative conference, Elon Musk predicted that by 2040 there would be at least 10 billion humanoid robots, with each priced between $20,000 and $25,000.

Do the math. According to Musk, this technology could be worth $250 trillion by 2040.

Put another way, that’s roughly equal to:

  • 175 Teslas
  • 107 Amazons
  • 140 Metas
  • 84 Googles
  • 65 Microsofts
  • And 55 Nvidias

And here’s the wild part — this $250 trillion wave isn’t tied to one company, but to an entire ecosystem of AI innovators set to reshape the global economy.

It’s a leap so massive, it could reshape how businesses, governments, and consumers operate worldwide.

Even if that $250 trillion figure sounds ambitious, major firms like PwC and McKinsey still see AI unlocking multi-trillion-dollar potential.

How could anything be worth that much?

The answer lies in a breakthrough so powerful it’s redefining how humanity works, learns, and creates.

And this breakthrough has already set off a frenzy among hedge funds and Wall Street’s top investors.

What most investors don’t realize is that one under-owned company holds the key to this $250 trillion revolution.

In fact, Verge argues this company’s supercheap AI technology should concern rivals.

Before I reveal the details, let’s talk about how some of the richest people on the planet are positioning themselves.

  • Bill Gates sees artificial intelligence as the “biggest technological advance in my lifetime,” more transformative than the internet or personal computer, capable of improving healthcare, education, and addressing climate change.
  • Larry Ellison — through Oracle, is spending billions on Nvidia chips and partnering with Cohere to embed generative AI across Oracle’s cloud and apps.
  • Warren Buffett — not known for tech hype — says this breakthrough could have a ‘hugely beneficial social impact.

When billionaires from Silicon Valley to Wall Street line up behind the same idea — you know it’s worth paying attention to.

Even as we admire what Tesla, Nvidia, Alphabet, and Microsoft have built, we believe an even greater opportunity lies elsewhere…

But the real story isn’t Nvidia — it’s a much smaller company quietly improving the critical technology that makes this entire revolution possible.

And judging by what I’m hearing from both Silicon Valley insiders and Wall Street veterans…

This prediction might not be bold at all:

articles.title: Xcel Energy dividend and investment plan, regulated utility stock in focus
articles.summary: Xcel Energy Inc. has outlined its updated dividend framework and capital investment program. As a regulated electric and gas utility operating in several U.S. states, its shares are closely watched by income-focused investors. The company's stable regulated model provides pred...
articles.content:
By Thomas Klein, Operations & Strategy desk. Reviewed prior to publication on 2026-06-26, 20:33.

Xcel Energy Inc (US98389B1008) operates as a major regulated electric and gas utility group in the United States. The company is listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) and is widely followed by income-oriented investors as a dividend-paying stock.

Regulated utility with multi-state footprint

Xcel Energy generates, transmits and distributes electricity and natural gas to customers in several U.S. states, including Colorado, Minnesota, Wisconsin, Michigan, North Dakota, South Dakota, Texas and New Mexico, under a regulated utility model that ties returns to approved tariffs. The company serves millions of customers across these territories and operates through utility subsidiaries that hold the relevant state-level franchises and regulatory approvals.

The regulated framework typically allows Xcel Energy to recover prudently incurred costs and earn an allowed return on equity, subject to periodic rate cases before state commissions and the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission. This structure often results in relatively predictable cash flows, which support the company’s dividend policy and capital investment plans in areas such as grid modernization and generation capacity.

Dividend profile and earnings drivers

Xcel Energy has historically positioned itself as a dividend-paying utility, using its regulated earnings base to support regular cash distributions to shareholders. The company’s ability to maintain and grow the dividend depends on factors such as approved rate increases, customer growth, cost control and capital structure management, all of which feed into earnings per share over time.

Key earnings drivers include the size and timing of approved capital expenditure programs, the pace of renewable generation build-out and the evolution of fuel and purchased power costs relative to recovery mechanisms embedded in rates. Weather patterns and underlying demand trends in the company’s service territories can also influence volumes and capacity utilization, which in turn affect revenue and earnings within the regulatory constructs.

What the company sells and how it earns money

Xcel Energy primarily earns money by providing electricity and natural gas to residential, commercial and industrial customers through its regulated utility subsidiaries, billing customers for energy usage, capacity, and service charges approved by regulators. The company also invests in generation assets, including renewable energy projects, and recovers those investments over time through regulated rates tied to its asset base and allowed returns.

Where the stock trades today

Xcel Energy Inc shares trade on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker XEL, quoted in U.S. dollars; the stock represents a large-cap regulated utility holding with a primary listing in the United States.

articles.title: Why Biogen (BIIB) Stock Is Up Today
articles.summary: Shares of Biogen (BIIB) surged 5.6% following several positive developments. These include the acquisition of RayThera Inc., an FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation for its drug Salanersen, and a recent analyst upgrade to 'Buy' from Needham. The company is also realigning its...
articles.content:
What Happened?

Shares of biotech company Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB) jumped 5.6% in the afternoon session after investors continued to cheer a string of growth signals, including the $1 billion purchase of RayThera Inc., an FDA Breakthrough Therapy tag for its drug candidate Salanersen, and a recent analyst upgrade.

The acquisition of RayThera is set to bolster Biogen's immunology pipeline. The positive sentiment was further supported by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration's 'Breakthrough Therapy' designation for Salanersen, a treatment for spinal muscular atrophy. This news, combined with a recent upgrade to 'Buy' from investment firm Needham and a strong first-quarter earnings beat, reinforced investor confidence. Additionally, Biogen is integrating its multi-billion dollar purchase of Apellis by halting some research programs to focus on its key marketed drugs, Empaveli and Syfovre, demonstrating a clear strategy to prioritize high-value assets.

Is now the time to buy Biogen? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free.

What Is The Market Telling Us

Biogen’s shares are not very volatile and have only had 6 moves greater than 5% over the last year. In that context, today’s move indicates the market considers this news meaningful, although it might not be something that would fundamentally change its perception of the business.

The biggest move we wrote about over the last year was 10 months ago when the stock gained 4.8% on the news that the company and its partner Eisai received FDA approval for a weekly subcutaneous injection of their Alzheimer's drug, Leqembi, for maintenance dosing.

The new, more convenient formulation, which will be marketed as Leqembi Iqlik, allows for at-home administration for patients who have been on the drug for 18 months. This provides an alternative to the previous hour-long intravenous infusions. Adding to the positive momentum, Biogen, along with partner Stoke Therapeutics, presented promising data for zorevunersen at the 36th International Epilepsy Congress. The data showed durable seizure reductions in patients with Dravet syndrome, a severe form of epilepsy, supporting the drug's potential as a first-in-class disease-modifying treatment.

Biogen is up 19.7% since the beginning of the year, and at $212.80 per share, it has set a new 52-week high. Despite the year-to-date gain, investors who bought $1,000 worth of Biogen’s shares 5 years ago would now be looking at only $625.39.

ONE MORE THING: The $21 AI Application Stock Wall Street Forgot. While Wall Street obsesses over who’s building AI, one company is already using it to print money. And nobody’s paying attention.

AI chip stocks trade at ridiculous valuations. This company processes a trillion consumer signals monthly using AI and trades at a third of the price. The gap won’t last. The institutions will figure it out. You need to see this first. Read the FREE Report Before They Notice.

articles.title: Argenx clinical pipeline and sector peers, shares watched in biotech
articles.summary: Argenx SE remains a key focus in the biotech sector due to its antibody pipeline, particularly in autoimmune diseases. Investors are closely monitoring its progress, clinical trial milestones, and competitive landscape against peers like Regeneron and Roche. The company's lead...
articles.content:
By Julia Schmitt, Sector & Peer Group desk. Reviewed prior to publication on 2026-06-26, 20:18.

Argenx SE (NL0010832176) continues to draw attention in the biotech space as investors monitor the progress of its antibody pipeline targeting autoimmune diseases. The company is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker ARGX, providing exposure to the broader biotech segment alongside peers such as Regeneron and Roche.

Biotech positioning and pipeline focus

Argenx SE is a biotechnology company developing antibody-based therapies for severe autoimmune diseases and cancer, with its lead drug candidate efgartigimod already approved in generalized myasthenia gravis in several markets. The company collaborates with partners and runs multiple clinical trials, expanding its reach in autoimmune neurology and hematology.

The biotech sector includes prominent players like Regeneron, Roche and Novartis, which also focus on immunology and oncology, highlighting the competitive landscape for antibody treatments. Argenx SE shares offer investors exposure to this innovative but volatile segment of the healthcare market.

Sector peers and investor interest

Biotech stocks such as Regeneron, Roche and Novartis have reported ongoing developments in their immunology and oncology pipelines, underlining continuous innovation in the sector. Argenx SE operates within this environment, where clinical outcomes and regulatory decisions can significantly influence investor sentiment.

Argenx SE shares trade on Nasdaq, enabling international investors to participate in the company’s progress alongside other biotech listings. The focus on autoimmune diseases and antibody engineering aligns the company with global efforts to address high unmet medical needs in neurology and hematology.

What the company develops

Argenx SE builds a portfolio of antibody-based therapies that modulate immune pathways in autoimmune diseases and cancer. The company’s work includes candidates designed to target specific proteins involved in disease processes, reflecting a focus on precision medicine within the biotech industry.

The listing and current trading venue

Argenx SE stock trades on Nasdaq in New York, giving international investors access to its biotech pipeline through a major US exchange.

This article was produced with AI assistance and editorially reviewed. Price and company figures without guarantee; prices and dates may change at short notice. No investment advice, no buy or sell recommendation. Stock-market transactions carry risks up to and including total loss.
